title: Phase D: Self-repo dogfood + 8 core decisions capture
scope: .fabric/knowledge/ (created on self-repo) + 8 KT-D decision baseline entries
description: Two-step dogfood combined into single commit `chore(dogfood): v2.0 baseline + key decisions`. Step 1: Run `pnpm build && node packages/cli/dist/index.js init` on fabric monorepo itself. Verify acceptance: `.fabric/knowledge/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes,pending}/` created; `agents.meta.json.counters` has KT >= 4; 4-7 KT-* baseline `.md` with full v2 frontmatter; `~/.fabric/knowledge/` auto-created; `fab doctor` exit 0. Step 2: Author KT-D `.md` entries for 8 core decisions during dogfood: (1) Boundary B (data + lifecycle + async-review primitive); rationale: rejected A=too thin, C=full-platform-wrong-fit. (2) v2.0 clean rebrand over v1.x staged; rationale: 0 users → migration tax = 0. (3) Dual-root layout (`~/.fabric` + `<repo>/.fabric`); rationale: gitignore can't filter by frontmatter. (4) stable_id `K[PT]-(D|P|G|M|PR)-NNNN` + monotonic counter; rationale: path-decoupled, layer-flip is only legal mutation. (5) 5-type / 3-maturity / 2-layer schema; rationale: flat scalars constraint from existing parser. (6) Review mode inference (not AskUserQuestion); rationale: AskUserQuestion is for genuine choices, mode is deducible. (7) Hook = reminder layer (exit 2 + stderr/followup_message); rationale: never block agent permanently. (8) Decay thresholds 90/30/14 days; rationale: 1/4 of Tencent article 12/6/N months — single-repo high-frequency scaling. Bug-handling protocol: hotfix on `2.0.0-rc.1` tag → `2.0.0-rc.1.fix.N`, NOT roll into rc.2.
estimated_time: 2-3h (Phase D budget); High due to integration validation across 6+ subsystem outputs

files.change: Init pipeline auto-creates the directory; manually author 8 KT-D-{0001..0008}.md entries — one per locked decision from grill-me Q&A. Each entry has v2 frontmatter (id, type=decision, maturity=accepted, layer=repo, title, tags, summary) + body explaining rationale + alternatives considered.
Init-scan produces baseline KT-G entries (e.g. 'use Zod strict schemas', 'prefer atomic writes'); minimum 1-2 entries
Init-scan produces baseline KT-M entries (e.g. 'KnowledgeEntry frontmatter shape', 'EventLedger discriminated union'); minimum 1-2 entries
Init-scan produces baseline KT-PR entries (e.g. 'init-scan 4-stage pipeline', 'review skill async lifecycle'); minimum 1-2 entries
Auto-created by init pipeline (empty initially) — async-review staging area
Init pipeline writes counters.KT.{D,P,G,M,PR} starting at issued count, monotonic; meta_reconciled_on_startup event emitted
Init pipeline ensures global root exists with same 6 subdirs structure

implementation: Run `pnpm build` at repo root — confirm exit 0
Run `node packages/cli/dist/index.js init` at fabric repo root — confirm 4-stage pipeline succeeds (forensic → init-scan → meta-bootstrap → client-config)
Verify directory structure: `ls .fabric/knowledge/` shows decisions/ pitfalls/ guidelines/ models/ processes/ pending/
Verify global root: `ls ~/.fabric/knowledge/` shows same 6 subdirs
Verify meta: `jq .counters.KT .fabric/agents.meta.json` returns object with at least 4 entries (D, G, M, PR likely)
Verify v2 frontmatter on baseline entries: spot-check 1-2 .md files have all 7 fields (id, type, maturity, layer, title, tags, summary)
Run `node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` — confirm exit 0 with no false-positives

Author 8 KT-D-NNNN.md decision entries (NNNN starts at next-counter via monotonic counter) — content from grill-me Q&A locked decisions, full v2 frontmatter, body with rationale + alternatives + tradeoffs
Run `pnpm test` and `pnpm lint` — confirm exit 0 (no regression from authoring entries)
Stage and commit: `git add .fabric/ && git commit -m 'chore(dogfood): v2.0 baseline + key decisions'`

reference.pattern: Self-hosting dogfood — fabric runs fabric on itself, baseline entries become living documentation
reference.examples: Each KT-D entry: 5-15 lines body, contract-style; reference grill-me Q&A id (Q1..Q7) for traceability

criteria: Directory `.fabric/knowledge/` exists with 6 subdirs: decisions, pitfalls, guidelines, models, processes, pending
`jq -r .counters.KT.D .fabric/agents.meta.json` returns numeric value ≥ 8 (not null, not undefined)
All 8 KT-D entries have full v2 frontmatter (id, type, maturity, layer, title, tags, summary) — verified by parsing each file with shared/schemas/agents-meta validator
`node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` exits 0 with no false-positives

Each of 8 decisions traces to a grill-me Q (id reference in body): Q1=clean-rebrand, Q2=drop-clients, Q3=dogfood, Q4=rc1-completion, Q5=release-hygiene, Q6=schema-forward-compat, Q7=phase-ordering — plus 8th decision is one of the 8 listed in Q7 not necessarily 1:1 to Q numbers
git commit with message `chore(dogfood): v2.0 baseline + key decisions` exists in repo history

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: rc.2 implementation features (fab_extract_knowledge, fabric-archive skill, Stop hooks), version bump or release artifacts (TASK-008 owns those), CHANGELOG entry (TASK-008). The dogfood RUN happens on the rc.1 codebase tag — only after this task succeeds will TASK-008 cut the actual release tag. Bug-handling: any bugs found during dogfood become 2.0.0-rc.1.fix.N hotfixes, NOT rolled into rc.2 scope.

rationale.chosen_approach: Combined dogfood-init + decision-capture into single commit (Q3+Q7 option B); 8-entry decision baseline as living documentation
alternatives_considered: Two separate commits (init result vs decisions)
decision_factors: Atomic commit shows 'fabric works on itself' as one shippable unit
8 decisions captures complete grill-me decision tree (no orphan rationale)
Pre-release dogfood validates init pipeline on a real-world non-trivial repo (not just test fixtures)
rationale.tradeoffs: Combined commit is larger (~10 new files) but reverting is also atomic; chose 8 over 5 for completeness — accept slightly more authoring effort

manual_checks: After init: visually inspect .fabric/knowledge/decisions/ — 8 .md files present, each well-formed
Run `cat .fabric/agents.meta.json | jq .counters.KT` — confirm D≥8, G/M/PR≥1 each
Pick a random .md from decisions/ — verify all 7 frontmatter fields present and non-empty
Run `pnpm test` after authoring — confirm no regressions
success_metrics: All 8 decisions captured with traceability to grill-me Q&A
Init pipeline e2e on self-repo exits 0
Counter monotonicity preserved (KT.D ≥ 8, all knowledge counters non-null)
unit: Frontmatter schema validation on each authored entry
integration: fab init e2e on fabric monorepo itself

risks.description: init pipeline bug discovered during self-repo dogfood (e.g. forensic.ts misclassifies a tsx file)
risks.mitigation: Treat as `2.0.0-rc.1.fix.N` hotfix path per Q3 — fix on rc.1 tag, do NOT roll into rc.2 scope; document in pitfalls/
risks.fallback: If init pipeline fails catastrophically, revert TASK-007 commit and ship rc.1 without dogfood baseline — accept worse marketing but unblock release

risks.description: Authoring 8 decision entries takes longer than 2-3h budget (each entry needs careful contract-style prose)
risks.mitigation: Use grill-me Q&A locked text verbatim as body — minimal rewriting; each entry ~10 lines max
risks.fallback: If time pressure, ship 5 critical decisions (option A) and defer 3 to rc.2.fix — but Q7 already locked option B

risks.description: Authored .md entries fail Zod frontmatter validation (typo in tags array, missing field)
risks.mitigation: Run `node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` after each batch of 2-3 entries — catches schema drift early
risks.fallback: Iterate per error message until validator exits 0

interfaces.name: KT-D entry frontmatter
interfaces.definition: { id: 'KT-D-NNNN', type: 'decision', maturity: 'accepted', layer: 'repo', title: string, tags: string[], summary: string }
